heading: Payment of insurance benefits in cash or debentures; borrowing money from Treasury to make payments
label: 1735d Payment of insurance benefits in cash or debentures; borrowing money from Treasury to make payments
notes: References in TextSubchapter IX–C of this chapter, referred to in subsec. (b), was classified to section 1749bbb et seq. of this title and was omitted from the Code.

Amendments1970—Subsec. (b)(2). provided for making payments for directly insured losses and made limitation provision applicable to such payments.Pub. L. 91–6091968—Subsec. (b). empowered the Secretary to borrow to make payments for reinsured losses under subchapter IX–C of this chapter, and limited such borrowing to $250,000,000 or such further sum as Congress may determine.Pub. L. 90–4481967—Subsecs. (a), (b). substituted “Secretary” for “Commissioner” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 90–19
amendments: Amendments1970—Subsec. (b)(2). provided for making payments for directly insured losses and made limitation provision applicable to such payments.Pub. L. 91–6091968—Subsec. (b). empowered the Secretary to borrow to make payments for reinsured losses under subchapter IX–C of this chapter, and limited such borrowing to $250,000,000 or such further sum as Congress may determine.Pub. children.label: a
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provisions of this chapter with respect to the payment of insurance benefits, the Secretary is authorized, in his discretion, to pay in cash or in debentures any insurance claim or part thereof which is paid on or after , on a mortgage or a loan which was insured under any section of this chapter either before or after such date. If payment is made in cash, it shall be in an amount equivalent to the face amount of the debentures that would otherwise be issued plus an amount equivalent to the interest which the debentures would have earned, computed to a date to be established pursuant to regulations issued by the Secretary.August 10, 1965

children.label: b
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to borrow from the Treasury from time to time such amounts as the Secretary shall determine are necessary (1) to make payments in cash (in lieu of issuing debentures guaranteed by the United States, as provided in this chapter) pursuant to the provisions of this section, and (2) to make payments for reinsured and directly insured losses under subchapter IX–C of this chapter: , That borrowings to make payments for reinsured and directly insured losses under subchapter IX–C shall be limited to $250,000,000 or such further sum as the Congress, by joint resolution, may from time to time determine. Notes or other obligations issued by the Secretary in borrowing under this subsection shall be subject to such terms and conditions as the secretary of the Treasury may prescribe. Each sum borrowed pursuant to this subsection shall bear interest at a rate determined by the Secretary of the Treasury, taking into consideration the average market yield on outstanding marketable obligations of the United States of comparable maturities during the month preceding the issuance of such notes or other obligations.11See References in Text note below.1Provided, however
